essence: Builds the physical world through strength, precision, and steady, hands‑on craftsmanship.

identity.title: Construction Worker
identity.summary: Performs physical labour on building sites, operates tools and machinery, follows plans, and supports the construction of structures and infrastructure.

salary.notes: Specialised roles (steel fixing, scaffolding, heavy machinery operation) can exceed £50k–£60k+. Overtime and site allowances can increase earnings.

qualifications: CSCS Card or Equivalent Site Certification
Knowledge of Tools, Materials, and Safety Procedures
Physical Fitness and Ability to Work Outdoors

lifestyle.description: Construction workers operate in a physically demanding, outdoor environment where teamwork and safety are essential. Days involve lifting materials, assembling structures, operating tools, and following instructions from supervisors or tradespeople. Weather, deadlines, and site conditions shape the rhythm of the work. The role requires stamina, coordination, and a strong respect for safety protocols. While the work can be tough and repetitive, it offers clear, tangible results — seeing a building rise from the ground is deeply satisfying. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy physical activity, practical tasks, and contributing to projects that shape communities.

habits: Communicates clearly with the site team
